blocks.content:
Congratulations on completing this path. You practiced the core loop many reliability teams use after a load test: anchor the window, compare external probes to generator metrics, classify errors in logs, then explain latency with traces.

You should now be able to:

- Explain why passing k6 thresholds alone is not a production guarantee
- Correlate k6 timing with synthetic metrics and application telemetry in one timeline
- Produce a short evidence-backed narrative for stakeholders

**More to explore (optional)**

- [Run your first k6 performance test](/docs/learning-paths/run-first-k6-test/)
- [Establish a performance baseline with k6](/docs/learning-paths/establish-k6-baseline/)
- [Use Metrics Drilldown to investigate high cardinality issues](/docs/learning-paths/drilldown-metrics/)
- [Use Logs Drilldown to quickly filter and visualize log data](/docs/learning-paths/drilldown-logs/)
- [Use Traces Drilldown to identify high latency service requests](/docs/learning-paths/drilldown-traces/)
